_header: confluent_gold_flow.json — parameterized IBM DataStage (PX) flow definition
that builds the Confluent GOLD marts from the Flink-written silver layer.
Location  : confluent/datastage/confluent_gold_flow.json
Repository: https://github.com/aseelert/ibmas-watsonxdata-dbt
Project   : watsonx.data · dbt · Spark · Confluent medallion demo
Author    : Alexander Seelert — IBM Customer Success Engineer
Copyright : (c) 2026 Alexander Seelert — demo asset, provided as-is.
Changelog :
v1.0 (2026-06-26) — Initial version. Parameterized pipeline-flow v3 graph
for confluent_gold_daily_sales (fully wired) plus documented placeholder
nodes for confluent_gold_category_performance and confluent_gold_customer_360.
IMPORTANT — THIS IS A BEST-EFFORT TEMPLATE, NOT A LIVE-VALIDATED EXPORT.
It follows the Common Pipeline 'pipeline-flow' v3 schema that the CP4D
DataStage REST API (POST /data_intg/v3/data_intg_flows) accepts, but it
has NOT been round-tripped against a running DataStage service. Stage IDs,
the connector 'op' names and the exact connector property keys may need to
be adjusted to match your CP4D version. Treat the SQL business logic (which
mirrors the dbt gold models exactly) as the contract; treat the connector
plumbing as a starting point to be opened and saved once in the DataStage
canvas so the service can normalize it.
PLACEHOLDERS (substituted by confluent/scripts/create_datastage_flow.py from .env):
@@PROJECT_ID@@        — WXD_DATASTAGE_PROJECT_ID
@@CATALOG@@           — WXD_CATALOG            (default iceberg_data)
@@SILVER_SCHEMA@@     — CONFLUENT_SILVER_SCHEMA (default confluent_demo_silver)
@@GOLD_SCHEMA@@       — CONFLUENT_GOLD_SCHEMA   (default confluent_demo_gold)
@@CONNECTION_REF@@    — guid of the watsonx.data / Presto connection asset
that already exists in the project (see README.md)
@@CONNECTION_NAME@@   — display name of that connection asset

comments.content: PARITY CONTRACT: this DataStage flow MUST produce byte-for-byte the same gold numbers as dbt's gold_daily_sales and Spark's spark_gold_daily_sales. The SQL pushed down below is copied verbatim from models/gold/gold_daily_sales.sql. Do not 'improve' it — the intentional count(distinct order_id) and decimal(14,2) casts are part of the contract.

datastage.description: Confluent GOLD builder (DataStage engine). Reads Flink-written confluent_demo_silver from watsonx.data and writes confluent_demo_gold marts. Functional twin of the Spark gold job.

ui_data.name: confluent_gold_daily_sales
ui_data.description: Mart 1 of 3 — daily_sales. Grain: (order_date, category). Built and wired in this template; the other two marts are documented as placeholder nodes below.

properties.comment: watsonx.data (Presto/Iceberg) connection asset. Reuses WXD_HOST / WXD_PORT / WXD_API_KEY exactly like the dbt + Spark paths.

usage.select_statement: SELECT order_date, category, order_id, quantity, net_amount, status FROM @@CATALOG@@.@@SILVER_SCHEMA@@.confluent_silver_sales_enriched

parameters.comment: Equivalent to: select order_date, category, count(distinct order_id) order_count, sum(quantity) units_sold, cast(sum(net_amount) as decimal(14,2)) net_revenue ... group by order_date, category. Verbatim from models/gold/gold_daily_sales.sql.

usage.create_statement: CREATE TABLE @@CATALOG@@.@@GOLD_SCHEMA@@.confluent_gold_daily_sales (order_date date, category varchar, order_count bigint, units_sold bigint, net_revenue decimal(14,2))

parameters.select_statement: SELECT category, sum(order_count) AS total_orders, sum(units_sold) AS total_units, cast(sum(net_revenue) AS decimal(14,2)) AS total_revenue, cast(sum(net_revenue) / nullif(sum(units_sold),0) AS decimal(14,2)) AS avg_revenue_per_unit FROM @@CATALOG@@.@@GOLD_SCHEMA@@.confluent_gold_daily_sales GROUP BY category ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
parameters.comment: Built ON TOP of confluent_gold_daily_sales (a roll-up), exactly like the dbt VIEW. Implement as a second Connector source (select above) -> target connector writing confluent_gold_category_performance.

parameters.status: TODO — wire with a LEFT join (Join stage, link-sort + left outer)
parameters.comment: Grain: one row per customer. Build a 'metrics' aggregate from confluent_silver_sales_enriched (count(distinct case when status=... then order_id end) per status; lifetime_value = coalesce(sum(case when status='completed' then net_amount else 0 end),0)), then LEFT JOIN confluent_silver_customers ON customer_id so customers with zero orders survive, coalescing the metric columns to 0. Two-input Join stage (left outer) + a Transformer for the coalesce().
